question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user craft malformed or consistently-failing transaction responses targeting specific nodes so that NodeSupplier.validateNode() removes those nodes from the CopyOnWriteArrayList, causing >= 30% of network processing nodes to be excluded from the active node pool without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unbounded .retry() operator in the publish() Flux to cause the monitor to repeatedly re-submit transactions to already-degraded nodes, amplifying load on a subset of nodes and causing >= 30% of them to become unresponsive and be removed from the active node list?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the onErrorContinue(PublishException.class, ...) handler in publish() to silently suppress node-level failures, preventing the monitor from detecting that >= 30% of nodes have stopped processing, thereby masking a partial network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the second onErrorContinue((t, r) -> log.error(...)) catch-all in publish() to silently absorb unexpected errors from >= 30% of nodes, causing the monitor to continue operating as if those nodes are healthy while they are actually shut down?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Schedulers.newParallel('publisher', publishProperties.getClients()) thread pool to become fully saturated by inducing slow responses from a subset of nodes, starving the remaining nodes of publisher threads and effectively shutting down >= 30% of node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Schedulers.newParallel('resolver', publishProperties.getResponseThreads()) thread pool exhaustion by causing response processing to hang for targeted nodes, preventing receipt/record resolution for >= 30% of nodes and effectively disabling their transaction processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .flatMapIterable(Function.identity()) step in publish() by causing the transactionGenerator.next(0) to return an oversized batch, flooding specific nodes with transactions and causing them to be marked as failed and removed from the active node list?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .parallel(publishProperties.getClients()) step in publish() combined with a fixed client count to cause all parallel slots to be occupied by requests to a subset of nodes, starving >= 30% of other nodes of any transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .doFinally(s -> log.warn('Stopped publisher after {} signal', s)) terminal handler in publish() by triggering a signal that terminates the publisher Disposable, causing the monitor to stop submitting transactions to all nodes and effectively shutting down >= 30% of monitored node processing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onErrorContinue((t, r) -> log.error(...)) in subscribe() to silently suppress mirror node subscription errors, causing the monitor to fail to detect that >= 30% of network nodes have stopped producing data, masking a partial network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .subscribeOn(Schedulers.parallel()) in subscribe() to cause the shared parallel scheduler to become saturated, preventing the monitor from receiving subscription responses from >= 30% of nodes and masking their shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .doFinally(s -> log.warn('Stopped subscribe after {} signal', s)) terminal handler in subscribe() by triggering a cancellation signal, causing the subscribe Disposable to be disposed and stopping all mirror node subscription monitoring, masking the shutdown of >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: kubernetesClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the KubernetesClientBuilder().build() auto-discovery mechanism in kubernetesClient() to inject a malicious kubeconfig or service account token, gaining access to the Kubernetes API and using it to delete or cordon >= 30% of network node pods without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: kubernetesClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConditionalOnCloudPlatform(CloudPlatform.KUBERNETES) condition in kubernetesClient() by spoofing Kubernetes platform detection environment variables, causing the KubernetesClient bean to be instantiated with attacker-controlled cluster credentials and used to shut down >= 30% of node pods?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: kubernetesClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the KubernetesClient bean created by kubernetesClient() to enumerate and drain Kubernetes nodes hosting >= 30% of network processing nodes, causing those nodes to be evicted and shut down without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConditionalOnProperty(value = 'hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.enabled', havingValue = 'true', matchIfMissing = true) condition in publish() by manipulating externalized configuration (e.g., via Spring Cloud Config or environment variable injection) to disable publishing, causing the monitor to stop sending transactions to all nodes and masking the shutdown of >= 30% of them?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConditionalOnProperty(value = 'hiero.mirror.monitor.subscribe.enabled', havingValue = 'true', matchIfMissing = true) condition in subscribe() by manipulating externalized configuration to disable subscriptions, causing the monitor to stop detecting that >= 30% of nodes have shut down?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeSupplier.get() round-robin counter (counter.getAndIncrement() % nodes.size()) by causing nodes to be removed from the CopyOnWriteArrayList mid-iteration, triggering an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException that disrupts transaction routing to >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeSupplier.validateNode() logic by causing the TransferTransaction validation to return a non-SUCCESS status for >= 30% of nodes, triggering nodes.remove(node) for each and effectively shutting down those nodes from the monitor's perspective?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeSupplier.validateNode() TimeoutException handler to cause validation timeouts for >= 30% of nodes by introducing network-level delays, causing those nodes to be removed from the active list via nodes.remove(node) without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeSupplier.refresh() Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...) retry policy to cause the monitor to enter an infinite retry loop when the address book returns a manipulated node list, preventing >= 30% of legitimate nodes from being added to the active pool?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeSupplier.getAddressBook() REST API call to inject a manipulated address book response that omits >= 30% of legitimate nodes, causing those nodes to never be validated or added to the active node pool?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeSupplier.toNodeProperties() filter (.filter(s -> tlsPredicate.test(s.getPort()))) by manipulating service endpoint port values in the address book response to cause >= 30% of nodes to be filtered out by the TLS predicate, effectively removing them from the active node pool?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeSupplier.toNodeProperties() .limit(nodeValidation.getMaxEndpointsPerNode()) cap to cause the monitor to only use a single endpoint per node, and then by making that endpoint unreachable, cause >= 30% of nodes to fail validation and be removed from the active list?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TransactionPublisher.toClient() network deduplication logic (Collectors.toMap(..., (a, b) -> a)) by causing multiple nodes to share the same service endpoint in the address book, causing the merge function to silently drop >= 30% of nodes from the client network map?
